agent1 (Round 2 summarized): Clinical staging of hepatocellular carcinoma relies solely on factors such as tumor size, number of lesions, vascular involvement, and extrinsic spread—regardless of histologic differentiation. Well-differentiated tumors indicate more benign biologically but do not automatically qualify as clinically early since clinical stages depend only on anatomical features, not microscopic characteristics.

Final Answer
\boxed{B}

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
